skill.name: conductor-manage
skill.description: Manage track lifecycle: archive, restore, delete, rename, and cleanup
skill.summary: A Claude skill for managing Conductor workflow tracks including archiving, restoring, deleting, renaming, and cleaning up orphaned artifacts.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 208 static findings are false positives. The skill consists of documentation files (SKILL.md and implementation-playbook.md) - no executable code. The detected patterns are markdown formatting (backticks for file paths), documentation text (words containing 'des' misinterpreted as weak crypto), and example error messages misinterpreted as reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Commands False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' but these are markdown backticks used for code formatting in documentation, not actual shell command execution. Files scanned are SKILL.md and implementation-playbook.md - both are documentation files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Documentation files containing markdown formatting with backticks for file paths and commands, not actual shell execution code

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner misidentified 'des' substring in words like 'description', 'destructive', 'described' as weak DES cryptographic algorithm
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: String pattern matching false positive - 'des' in documentation text is not cryptographic code

dangerous_patterns.title: System/Network Reconnaissance False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner misidentified example error message templates and track ID patterns as reconnaissance activity
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Example error messages and track ID format documentation, not actual reconnaissance code

content.value_statement: This skill automates track lifecycle management for Conductor workflows, handling archiving, restoration, deletion, renaming, and cleanup of orphaned artifacts across active, completed, and archived states.
actual_capabilities: Archive completed tracks to free up active workspace
Restore archived tracks back to active state
Permanently delete tracks with confirmation safety
Rename tracks while maintaining metadata consistency
Detect and clean up orphaned track artifacts
List and filter tracks by status (active, completed, archived)
limitations: Requires Conductor to be initialized in the repository
Cannot modify tracks without proper file permissions
Does not execute track tasks - only manages lifecycle

use_cases.title: Archive completed workflow tracks
use_cases.description: Move finished tracks to archive storage to keep active workspace organized
use_cases.target_user: Project managers and developers managing multiple workflow tracks

use_cases.title: Restore archived tracks for review
use_cases.description: Bring back previously archived tracks to active state for reference or continued work
use_cases.target_user: Team leads reviewing past project completions

use_cases.title: Clean up orphaned artifacts
use_cases.description: Detect and remove track files that no longer have corresponding entries in tracks.md

prompt_templates.title: List all tracks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the conductor-manage skill to list all tracks in my Conductor workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview of all tracks and their statuses

prompt_templates.title: Archive a completed track
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the conductor-manage skill to archive the completed track with ID 'feature-login_20250115'
prompt_templates.scenario: Archiving a track after completing all tasks

prompt_templates.title: Restore an archived track
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the conductor-manage skill to restore the archived track 'bug-fix_20241201'
prompt_templates.scenario: Bringing back an archived track for further work

prompt_templates.title: Cleanup orphaned artifacts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the conductor-manage skill to detect and cleanup any orphaned track artifacts
prompt_templates.scenario: Regular maintenance to keep track directory clean

output_examples.output:

- [ ] dashboard-ui_20250115 - Pending

Move to archive?

Cleanup Complete:
Orphaned directories removed: 2
Missing metadata entries: 0
Tracks updated in tracks.md: 0

best_practices: Always backup track data before performing delete operations
Confirm destructive actions like delete and cleanup before execution
Use the --force flag only when you are certain of the operation
Keep archived tracks for audit trail and historical reference
anti_patterns: Using delete without confirming which track will be removed
Skipping the archive step before permanent deletion
Running cleanup without understanding what orphaned means
Renaming tracks without updating all references to the old ID

faq.question: What is Conductor?
faq.answer: Conductor is a workflow management system that helps track projects, tasks, and goals using markdown files and directories.

faq.question: Can I recover a deleted track?
faq.answer: No, permanent deletion cannot be undone. Always use archive instead of delete unless you are certain.

faq.question: What are orphaned artifacts?
faq.answer: Orphaned artifacts are track directories that exist but no longer have a corresponding entry in tracks.md.

faq.question: How do I rename a track safely?
faq.answer: Use the rename function which automatically updates all references in tracks.md and metadata files.

faq.question: Does this skill execute track tasks?
faq.answer: No, this skill only manages the track lifecycle (archive, restore, delete, rename, cleanup). Use other skills to execute actual track work.

faq.question: What happens to git history when I delete a track?
faq.answer: Git commit history is preserved. Only the track directory and its contents are removed.
